rationale: At 389fcc41, the round-1 boundary blocker is fixed: TestEnforcementHasNoGitLeaks passes, internal/approve has no gitutil import, and the named fences are zero-hit. Build, vet, formatting, validation, and the spec-115 integration surface pass. The CI-equivalent short whole-tree suite reports only the disclosed worktree-state failure in internal/instruct TestRun_IdleNoBeads; all other packages, including internal/harness, pass. The unbounded whole-tree run entered the disclosed non-short harness stall and emitted no additional failure before review interruption. golangci-lint reports exactly the two pre-existing G115 findings in internal/journal/lock_unix.go. Neither disclosed condition is introduced by spec 115. No new spec-115 CI or integration regression was found under the corrected scope.

findings.detail: go test ./internal/lint/ -run TestEnforcementHasNoGitLeaks -v passes; internal/approve's import list contains no gitutil; BranchExistsE and show-ref searches return zero hits; origin/main diff for internal/gitutil is empty.

go test -short ./... fails only internal/instruct TestRun_IdleNoBeads due to the active spec worktree; every other package passes. The requested unbounded go test ./... stalled in the disclosed non-short harness path and was interrupted after emitting no new failure.
golangci-lint reports only the two disclosed pre-existing gosec G115 conversions in internal/journal/lock_unix.go (mindspec-8ud6).
go build ./..., go vet ./..., and gofmt -l ./cmd ./internal pass/are empty. mindspec validate spec 115-impl-approve-panel-gate exits zero with only the review-worktree lifecycle-binding warning.
